segments.p1-9-g45: Pronoun Я is the nominative subject. Согласен is a short-form adjective (predicate) in the masculine singular meaning “agree”/“in agreement”; together they form the clause “I agree.” The colon after it signals the speaker is about to elaborate.
segments.p1-9-g46: Человек (subject) is followed by the copula есть (present tense ‘to be’) linking to the predicate noun животное. Here есть is the explicit copula (often omitted in modern Russian) and животное is a predicate noun in the nominative meaning “an animal.”
segments.p1-9-g47: По преимуществу is an adverbial phrase meaning “for the most part / predominantly.” Созидающее is a present active participle (neuter nominative) agreeing with the nearest noun животное (neuter), so it modifies “animal” as “a predominantly creative animal.”
segments.p1-9-g48: Принужденное is a short passive participle (neuter nominative) continuing to modify животное: “forced.” The following infinitives стремиться and заниматься are the verbs governed by that participle: “forced to strive (стремиться) toward a goal (к цели) consciously (сознательно) and to engage (заниматься) in engineering craft (инженерным искусством).” Note that заниматься governs the instrumental case (инженерным искусством).
segments.p1-9-g49: То есть is the explanatory phrase “that is.” Вечно и беспрерывно are adverbs modifying the infinitive прокладывать (“to lay/forge”). Дорогу (accusative) is the direct object, себе is a dative reflexive indicating beneficiary (“for himself”), and прокладывать is the infinitive. Хотя ... куда бы то ни было is an idiomatic concessive construction meaning “even to wherever/anywhere at all,” with бы as the modal/conditional particle and ни ... было forming the emphatic “at all.”
segments.p1-9-g50: Но вот именно потому-то is a clustered, emphatic introductory phrase: Но = 'but/however' sets contrast; вот + именно intensify ('precisely/just'); потому is 'for that reason' and the enclitic -то on потому (потому-то) adds emphatic/pointing force ('precisely for that reason'). It prepares the following causal clause.
segments.p1-9-g51: может быть is a parenthetical modal phrase meaning 'perhaps' or 'it may be'; грамматически it's an impersonal modal made of мочь + быть and softens the assertion that follows.
segments.p1-9-g52: ему и хочется иногда вильнуть в сторону is an impersonal/dative construction: 'ему хочется' = 'he feels like' (literally 'to him it is desired'); the particle и adds slight emphasis/contrast ('even/also'); иногда = 'sometimes'; вильнуть в сторону is an infinitive meaning 'to swerve aside' or 'to duck off to the side.'
segments.p1-9-g53: что он присужден пробивать эту дорогу introduces the explanatory clause with что = 'that'; присужден is a literary short passive/participial form from присудить used here in the sense 'is adjudged/condemned/destined' and is followed by an infinitive (пробивать), so the meaning is 'he is fated/condemned to break through'; пробивать эту дорогу = 'to force/drive this road (i.e. to clear/make it by persistent effort).'
segments.p1-9-g54: да еще is a colloquial connective meaning 'and besides' or 'moreover' (да here = 'and/yet'); it adds an extra reason or reinforcement to what follows.
segments.p1-9-g55: пожалуй is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose' and softens/asserts uncertainty about the following statement.
segments.p1-9-g56: потому is the causal 'because/for that reason' and here begins a new because-clause (потому, что ...).
segments.p1-9-g57: что как ни глуп непосредственный деятель вообще is a concessive phrase introduced by как ни ... (literally 'however [adjective] ...'), so 'however stupid the immediate doer may be in general'; note глуп (predicative adjective) appears before the noun phrase непоcредственный деятель — a somewhat literary ordering — and вообще = 'in general.'
segments.p1-9-g58: но все-таки ему иногда приходит на мысль is another concessive turn: но всё-таки = 'but nevertheless/still'; ему иногда приходит на мысль is an impersonal expression 'it sometimes occurs to him' (dative ему + приходит на мысль = 'a thought comes to him').
segments.p1-9-g59: что дорога-то introduces a highlighted topic with the particle -то attached to дорога (дорога-то) to contrast/emphasize 'the road (now)'; что = 'that' begins the reported thought.
segments.p1-9-g60: оказывается is an impersonal/present-tense verb meaning 'it turns out' or 'apparently' and signals that what follows is a discovered fact or realization.
segments.p1-9-g61: почти всегда идет куда бы то ни было и что главное дело не в том combines narration and another subordinate clause: почти всегда идет = 'almost always goes/proceeds'; куда бы то ни было is a fixed concessive/indefinite phrase meaning 'to wherever/anywhere' (бы то ни было are particles forming 'any'); and далее и что главное дело не в том = 'and that the main thing is not in that' (не в том = 'not in that/that is not the point').
segments.p1-9-g62: куда она идет is the relative clause 'where it (the road) goes' with куда = 'to where'; она refers back to дорога (feminine) and идет = 'goes/proceeds.'
segments.p1-9-g63: а в том is the contrasting pivot of the proverb-like construction не в том ... а в том = 'not in that ... but in that'; а = 'but' and в том = 'in that' (referring to the following content clause).
segments.p1-9-g64: чтоб она только шла и чтоб благонравное дитя introduces two parallel subjunctive/optative clauses introduced by чтоб (in order that/that): чтоб она только шла = a desiderative 'that it would only/just go on' (чтобы + past-form expresses wish/optative); и чтоб благонравное дитя = 'and that the well‑behaved child...', preparing the next clause about the child.
segments.p1-9-g65: пренебрегая инженерным искусством is a gerundial phrase (деепричастие) 'neglecting/ignoring engineering art/skill' that modifies 'дитя' (explains the manner/attitude of the child); инженерным искусством is instrumental case, 'by/with engineering art/skill.'
segments.p1-9-g66: не предавалось губительной праздности uses the past/reflexive verb предаваться in the subjunctive/desiderative construction after чтоб: 'should not give itself up to ruinous idleness' — не negates the verb, губительной is adjectival (ruinous) modifying праздности (idleness); syntactically, it's 'чтоб ... не предавалось ...' = 'that ... should not give itself up to ...'.
segments.p1-9-g67: которая is the feminine relative pronoun 'which/that' referring back to праздности (feminine) and introducing the relative clause that follows.
segments.p1-9-g68: как известно is a parenthetical phrase 'as is known' or 'as everyone knows' (как = 'as', известно = 'is known/well-known') used to signal that the following statement is a commonplace.
segments.p1-9-g69: есть мать всех пороков is the concluding copular clause 'is the mother of all vices': есть is the emphatic present copula 'is', мать = 'mother' used metaphorically, всех = genitive plural 'of all', пороков = 'vices' in genitive plural.
segments.p1-9-g70: Человек is a generic subject meaning ‘man’ or ‘a person’. Любит + infinitive expresses a habitual inclination (literally ‘loves to…’); here two infinitives are coordinated: созидать and (with its object) дороги прокладывать. Дороги is the direct object of прокладывать in the accusative plural, so the phrase literally reads ‘loves to create and to lay out roads.’
segments.p1-9-g71: Это is a demonstrative/dummy pronoun serving as the subject (‘this’/‘that’), and бесспорно is a predicative adverb meaning ‘indisputable’ or ‘unquestionable’. Together they form a short independent clause: ‘This is indisputable.’
segments.p1-9-g72: Но is a contrastive conjunction ‘but’; отчего is an interrogative adverb ‘why/for what reason’, with the particle же adding emphasis. Он is the subject. До страсти is an adverbial phrase with до + genitive meaning ‘to the point of passion’ (an intensifier modifying любит). Любит is present tense ‘loves/likes’; тоже (‘also’) links this preference to what was just said (he not only creates but also loves destruction). Разрушение и хаос are compound objects of любит (‘destruction and chaos’).
segments.p1-9-g33: Ведь is an emphatic particle meaning 'after all' that frames the whole sentence as a reminder or objection; это is the demonstrative pronoun acting as the nominal predicative ('this is ...'); покамест, ещё, and только are stacked adverbs/modifiers (покамест = 'for the time being', ещё = 'still/yet', только = 'only/just') that together qualify the predicative, producing the sense 'so far only/just one'; ваше предположение is the possessive + noun 'your assumption', with no copula because Russian omits 'to be' in the present tense.
segments.p1-9-g34: Положим is a parenthetical/hortative form from положить used like 'let us suppose' or 'assume (for argument's sake)'; it does not mean literal 'put' here but introduces a hypothetical concession.
segments.p1-9-g35: Что introduces the subordinate clause 'that ...'; это again is the demonstrative pronoun/predicative 'this (is)'; закон логики is a noun phrase where закон is the head 'law/principle' and логики (genitive) specifies 'of logic' — together 'a law/principle of logic'.
segments.p1-9-g36: Но is the coordinating conjunction 'but/however' that contrasts the following possibility with the preceding concession.
segments.p1-9-g37: Можно считать the pair может быть (here split into может + быть) is a parenthetical modal expression meaning 'perhaps/may be'; грамматически это модальность, not a full verb phrase inflecting the clause.
segments.p1-9-g38: Вовсе не is a strong negation 'not at all', with не negating the following noun; человечества is the genitive singular 'of humanity' — the phrase means 'not at all (a law) of humanity' (i.e. perhaps it is logical but not human).
segments.p1-9-g39: Вы is the second-person pronoun (plural or polite singular) addressing the addressees; the comma after it sets it off from the following parenthetical.
segments.p1-9-g40: Может быть is again a parenthetical modal 'perhaps' here (может expresses possibility and быть completes the idiom), softening the question that follows.
segments.p1-9-g41: Думаете is the 2nd person plural present of думать 'you think' and carries the main verbal action of the question; its form (plural) matches the polite/plural Вы used earlier.
segments.p1-9-g42: Господа is a vocative/address 'gentlemen' inserted as an interjection to address the audience directly; its placement between commas is typical for direct address in Russian.
segments.p1-9-g43: Что introduces the content clause 'that I am insane'; я is the nominative subject 'I'; сумасшедший is the predicate adjective 'insane' — there is no copula in the present tense, so the clause literally reads 'that I insane?'.
segments.p1-9-g44: Позвольте is the polite imperative (2nd person plural) 'allow/permit (me/us)'; оговориться is a reflexive verb here meaning 'to qualify/ make a reservation / state a clarification' (not 'misspeak') — together the phrase is a polite request to add a qualification.
segments.p1-9-g91: A bare comma segment; punctuation separating the preceding list from the short concluding formula.
segments.p1-9-g92: И проч. is an abbreviated colloquial phrase meaning “and so on / and the like.” И is the coordinating conjunction “and,” while проч. is short for прочее/прочие and functions like “etc.”; the whole functions as a trailing shorthand to indicate additional similar items.
segments.p1-9-g93: Вот is a pointing particle meaning “here/there/behold” used to introduce or emphasize what follows. Муравьи is nominative plural (the subject, 'ants'). Совершенно is an adverb modifying другого, meaning “completely/entirely.” Другого вкуса is a genitive noun phrase functioning as a predicate (literally ‘of different taste’), with другого (an adjective in genitive) qualifying вкус/вкуса; the copula 'are' is omitted in Russian and understood in English.
segments.p1-9-g94: У них есть is the existential/possessive construction meaning 'they have' (literally 'at them there is'). Одно удивительное здание is the quantified noun phrase 'one remarkable building' serving as the thing possessed; в этом же роде is a prepositional phrase modifying здание ('of this same kind'), where же is the emphatic/identifying particle reinforcing 'this same'.
segments.p1-9-g95: Навеки is an adverb meaning 'forever/for all time.' Нерушимое is a neuter adjective (agreeing with здание) meaning 'indestructible' or 'inviolable'; together they form an adjectival phrase further qualifying the building.
segments.p1-9-g96: The dash introduces an appositive: муравейник renames the 'building.' It is nominative and functions as an explicit label: '— an anthill.'
segments.p1-9-g97: С муравейника is a prepositional phrase with с + genitive meaning 'from / starting at the anthill.' Достопочтенные муравьи is the nominative subject 'the venerable/respectable ants.' Начали is the past-tense plural of начать 'to begin' (they began), matching the plural subject.
segments.p1-9-g98: Муравейником here is the same noun in the instrumental case; it functions adverbially/appositively as an echo of the starting point — 'with/by the anthill' or 'ending with the anthill' (the instrumental marks means or accompaniment). The repetition is rhetorical.
segments.p1-9-g99: Наверно is a modal adverb meaning 'probably' or 'I suppose,' marking the clause as conjectural.
segments.p1-9-g100: И is the coordinating 'and,' linking this verb to the earlier phrase. Кончат is the 3rd-person plural future (perfective) of кончить meaning 'will finish/end' (not 'die' here); it predicts that they will conclude with the anthill. Word order omits an explicit object because the instrumental муравейником supplies it.
segments.p1-9-g101: Что introduces a relative/result clause ('which'), referring back to the fact just stated. Приносит is present-tense 'brings' or 'bestows' (generalizing present). Большую честь is the accusative object 'great honour,' and их постоянству и положительности is a dative phrase 'to their constancy and positiveness' indicating who receives the honour.
segments.p1-9-g1: Господа is a vocative/plural form used to address the audience: 'Gentlemen' or 'sirs.' It stands alone with a following comma as an opening address.
segments.p1-9-g2: Я is the nominative personal pronoun 'I', the grammatical subject of the clause; the trailing comma marks a slight pause for the parenthetical 'конечно'.
segments.p1-9-g3: Конечно is a sentence/adverbial parenthetical meaning 'of course' or 'certainly'; it is set off by commas and modifies the whole clause 'I joke'.
segments.p1-9-g4: Шучу is first-person singular present indicative of шутить: 'I joke' / 'I'm joking.' It is the main verb of this clause and is simple present tense used conversationally.
segments.p1-9-g5: И links the next clause ('I myself know'); сам is an emphatic reflexive/adversative 'myself' (emphasizing the subject); знаю is first-person singular present 'I know.' Together: 'and I myself know...'.
segments.p1-9-g6: Что introduces a subordinate (object) clause of 'знаю' meaning 'that'; неудачно is an adverb modifying шучу meaning 'unsuccessfully/awkwardly'; шучу again is 1st-person present 'I joke.' The clause reads 'that I joke awkwardly/unsuccessfully.'
segments.p1-9-g7: Но is the contrasting conjunction 'but'; ведь is a pragmatic particle 'after all/you see' adding justification; и here adds slight emphasis ('and/yet'); нельзя is an impersonal modal meaning 'it is not permitted/one cannot' (no grammatical subject); же is an emphatic particle reinforcing нельзя; все is the accusative pronoun 'everything'; принимать за is a fixed verb phrase 'to take/consider as' where принимать is the verb and за is the preposition; шутку is the accusative object 'a joke.' Together: 'but after all one cannot take everything for a joke.'
segments.p1-9-g8: Я is the nominative subject pronoun 'I', marked off by a comma because of the parenthetical 'может быть'.
segments.p1-9-g9: Может быть is a fixed modal phrase meaning 'perhaps/maybe.' Grammatically it's an impersonal/modal expression indicating possibility; здесь it's parenthetical and set off by commas.
segments.p1-9-g10: Скрыпя is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'creaking/gnashing (while)...' and indicates a simultaneous manner of action; зубами is in the instrumental plural 'with/by (the) teeth', expressing the means; шучу is the main verb 'I joke.' Together they mean 'I joke while grinding/gnashing my teeth.'
segments.p1-9-g11: Господа is again an address-form (vocative/plural). Here it opens the sentence as a direct address to the listeners: 'Gentlemen.'
segments.p1-9-g12: Меня is the accusative form 'me' (the patient); мучат is third-person plural present 'they torment' where the grammatical subject is 'вопросы' (questions); вопросы is nominative plural 'questions.' Word order places the object before the verb for emphasis: 'Questions torment me.'
segments.p1-9-g13: Разрешите is an imperative, polite/plural form of разрешить meaning 'allow' or 'permit'; их is the accusative plural pronoun 'them' (referring to вопросы); мне is the dative 'to me' or 'for me' indicating the beneficiary. The construction 'разрешите их мне' functions as 'allow me to (deal with) them' or 'let me resolve them.'
segments.p1-9-g14: Вот here functions as a discourse marker ('now/see/so') that draws attention to the example; вы is the nominative second-person plural/formal 'you' and is the subject of the following verbs.
segments.p1-9-g15: Например is an adverb meaning 'for example' and sets up the instance that follows; it is parenthetical and modifies the whole proposition.
segments.p1-9-g16: Человека is the accusative singular 'a person/man' (direct object); от + старых привычек is a prepositional phrase meaning 'from old habits' (старых is genitive plural modifying привычек); хотите is second-person plural present 'you want'; отучить is the infinitive 'to wean/break (a habit)'; и coordinates a second infinitive phrase; волю его is the accusative object 'his will' (волю in accusative, его 'his' placed after for emphasis); исправить is the infinitive 'to correct/reform.' Syntactically, two infinitives governed by хотите: 'you want to wean a person from old habits and (to) reform his will.'
segments.p1-9-g17: Сообразно с + instrumental marks conformity: 'in accordance with' or 'according to.' Требованиями is instrumental plural 'requirements/demands' serving as the standard; науки is genitive 'of science'; и links to здравого смысла, where здравого (genitive singular) modifies смысл (genitive) to mean 'sound/common sense.' The whole prepositional phrase specifies the standard according to which you want to reform the person.
segments.p1-9-g18: Но — adversative conjunction introducing contrast. почему — interrogative adverb 'why/how come'. вы знаете — present tense, 2nd person plural (or polite singular) of знать: a direct question 'do you know'. The verb governs the subordinate clause that follows.
segments.p1-9-g19: что introduces the subordinate content clause ('that ...'). человека is the accusative singular of человек and names the person who would be acted on (object). не только is the first half of the correlative construction 'не только ... но и' ('not only ... but also'); можно is an impersonal modal ('it is possible/one can'), here applied to the action implied later (переделывать). The object (человека) precedes the impersonal predicate for emphasis.
segments.p1-9-g20: но и connects with не только to form 'not only ... but also'; и here emphasizes 'also/even'. нужно is another impersonal predicate meaning 'it is necessary' (stronger than можно). так modifies переделывать and means 'in that way/like that'. переделывать is the imperfective infinitive 'to remake/rework' (processual), used with the impersonal модals to say it's possible and necessary to do this.
segments.p1-9-g21: из чего is a prepositional + interrogative phrase 'from what' (asking the basis or evidence). вы заключаете is present tense, 2nd person plural of заключать in the sense 'to infer/conclude' and introduces the following content clause.
segments.p1-9-g22: что introduces the content clause. хотенью человеческому is dative ('to/for human desire') — older/poetic word order places the noun and adjective before the predicate; it indicates who/what is affected. так усиляет степень ('so'/'thus'). необходимо and надо are two impersonal modals placed together for emphasis ('it is necessary' / 'one must'); исправиться is the perfective reflexive infinitive 'to reform oneself / to be corrected', forming the predicate meaning 'it must/ought to be reformed'.
segments.p1-9-g23: Одним словом is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'in a word' or 'in short' used to summarize or preface a pithy restatement.
segments.p1-9-g24: почему вы знаете — repeated question 'why do you know', same present-tense construction as before; introduces the subordinate clause with what is supposedly known.
segments.p1-9-g25: что introduces the content clause. такое исправление — 'such a correction/reformation' (такое modifies исправление: 'of that kind'). действительно is an adverb adding emphasis ('indeed/actually'). принесет is the future tense of принести (perfective) 'will bring'. человеку is dative: 'to a person' (indirect object). выгоду is the accusative of выгода 'benefit/advantage' — the direct object of принесет.
segments.p1-9-g26: И — simple coordinating conjunction 'and', linking this sentence to what precedes.
segments.p1-9-g27: если уж все говорить is an idiomatic concessive phrase 'if (we are) to speak everything' → 'if we are going to be frank' or 'if I may say so'; если 'if', уж adds insistence/colloquial force, все here means 'everything' or 'at all', and говорить is the infinitive 'to speak'.
segments.p1-9-g28: почему вы так наверно убеждены — 'why are you so surely/certainly convinced': так modifies the degree, наверно here means 'certainly/surely' (archaic/spoken adverb), убеждены is the short-form plural adjective meaning 'convinced'.
segments.p1-9-g29: что не идти против настоящих — что introduces the subordinate clause; не идти против is an infinitival phrase 'not to go against' or 'not to oppose'; против takes the genitive. настоящих is the genitive plural adjective 'real/genuine' agreeing with the following noun (выгод).
segments.p1-9-g30: нормальных выгод — genitive plural 'normal advantages/benefits'; нормальных qualifies выгод and both are in the genitive because governed by против.
segments.p1-9-g31: гарантированных is a past passive participle in genitive plural agreeing with выгод: 'guaranteed'. доводами ... арифметикой are in the instrumental case and indicate the means or grounds by which they are guaranteed: 'by the arguments of reason and by arithmetic.'
segments.p1-9-g32: действительно для человека всегда выгодно states the impersonal/predicate clause: действительно 'indeed/really'; для человека is a dative phrase 'for man/for a person'; всегда 'always'; выгодно is a predicative adjective meaning 'advantageous/profitable' (it agrees as predicate with the situation). и есть закон для всего человечества adds a coordinate assertion: есть (present of быть) 'is' and закон 'a law/principle' 'for all humanity' (для всего человечества). Together this is a rhetorical question whether the practical rule is universally binding.
segments.p1-9-g73: Вот is an attention-getting/contrastive particle here (more like 'well' or 'now'); это is the demonstrative 'this' pointing to something just mentioned; скажите is the 2nd-person-plural (or polite singular) imperative 'tell' (polite/urgent request); -ка is a colloquial enclitic particle that softens or urges the imperative (like 'do tell' or 'why don't you'). Together they form a brusque request: 'Well then, tell me this!'.
segments.p1-9-g74: This is an impersonal construction: мне хочется + infinitive expresses desire/ inclination ('I feel like / I want to'). Об этом = 'about that/about this' (prepositional phrase). сам(ому) here is emphatic (emphasising 'I myself'); заявить is the infinitive 'to declare/say'; два слова = 'two words' used idiomatically for 'a couple of remarks'; особо modifies the whole phrase ('especially/particularly').
segments.p1-9-g75: Не ... ли forms a rhetorical question: 'Не потому ли' = 'Isn't it for that reason...?'. Потому = 'for that reason'; ли is an enclitic question particle marking the clause as interrogative.
segments.p1-9-g76: Может быть is a parenthetical modal phrase meaning 'perhaps' or 'maybe'; it's frequently used to soften a suggestion or hypothesis.
segments.p1-9-g77: Он = 'he'; так here is an intensifier meaning 'so' or 'so much' modifying любит; любит is present-tense 'loves/is fond of'; разрушение и хаос are the direct objects 'destruction and chaos'. The parenthesis begins with ведь 'after all / for' + это бесспорно 'this is indisputable' (an inserted justification).
segments.p1-9-g78: Что introduces a subordinate clause 'that it is indisputable that ...' (i.e. 'for it is undisputable that...'); иногда очень любит = 'sometimes loves very much' — the adverbs иногда (sometimes) and очень (very) modify любит.
segments.p1-9-g79: Это уж так is a colloquial parenthetical comment meaning roughly 'that's how it is / that's true' — уж adds insistence ('indeed'), так = 'so/that way'.
segments.p1-9-g80: The second что introduces the explanatory clause: 'that he himself instinctively fears ...'. Сам emphasises 'he himself'; инстинктивно = adverb 'instinctively'; боится takes infinitives as complements (боится достигнуть, довершить) meaning 'is afraid to reach/complete'. Созидаемое is a present-participial adjective 'being created' modifying здание, so 'the building he is creating.'
segments.p1-9-g81: Почем (archaic/colloquial form of почему) here means 'how / how come' — 'Почем вы знаете?' = 'How do you know?'; вы знаете is standard 2nd-person plural/formal 'you know'.
segments.p1-9-g82: Again, может быть is a parenthetical hedge meaning 'perhaps / maybe'.
segments.p1-9-g83: Он = 'he'; здание-то uses the postpositive particle -то (attached to здание) as an emphatic marker pointing to 'the building' specifically; любит только издали = 'loves it only from afar' — только 'only', издали is an adverb 'from a distance / from afar' (here used to contrast with being near).
segments.p1-9-g84: А introduces contrast ('but/whereas'); отнюдь не is a strong negation meaning 'by no means / not at all'; вблизи = 'up close / nearby'.
segments.p1-9-g85: Again a parenthetical maybe: может быть = 'perhaps'.
segments.p1-9-g86: Он только любит созидать его = 'he only likes to create / build it' — любит + infinitive созидать expresses liking for the action of creating; его refers back to the building.
segments.p1-9-g87: А не жить в нем = 'and not to live in it' — a simple negative contrast; в нем = prepositional phrase 'in it' (нем = 'in it').
segments.p1-9-g88: Предоставляя is a gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'leaving / granting / allowing' and links to the main clause: 'leaving it afterwards...'; его = 'it' (the building); потом = 'afterwards / later'.
segments.p1-9-g89: Как-то here functions idiomatically as 'as it were / for example / such as'; муравьям is the dative plural 'to ants' (used literally here and suggestively figuratively).
segments.p1-9-g90: Баранам = 'to rams' (dative plural) — the author lists animals to emphasize a dismissive fate for the building; и = 'and'; проч. (прочее) = abbreviation meaning 'etc.' or 'and so on'.
segments.p1-9-g144: 'Достижение' (noun in the nominative) is fronted for emphasis; 'он' is the nominative pronoun subject (3rd person singular); 'любит' is present tense indicative, 3rd person singular ('he likes' / 'he is fond of'). The word order (noun + pronoun + verb) puts stress on 'Достижение'.
segments.p1-9-g145: А is the contrastive conjunction 'but'; 'достигнуть' is the infinitive 'to attain/reach' used impersonally to refer back to the preceding idea; 'уж' is an emphatic/adverbial particle ('already' / 'anymore' here); 'и' functions as an intensifying particle ('even/also') before the negation; 'не' is the simple negation; 'совсем' is an adverb 'completely' — together 'уж и не совсем' = 'not quite anymore'.
segments.p1-9-g146: И here links the latter clause to the previous one ('and'); 'это' is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it' referring to the situation just described.
segments.p1-9-g147: Конечно is an adverbial parenthetical meaning 'of course' or 'naturally' and modifies the whole clause that follows.
segments.p1-9-g148: 'Ужасно' is an adverb here used as an intensifier ('terribly/very'); 'смешно' is a predicative (neuter form) meaning 'funny' or 'ridiculous'. Russian omits the copula 'is' in the present, so the phrase = 'is terribly funny.'
segments.p1-9-g149: Одним словом is an idiomatic instrumental phrase ('in one word' = 'in a word' / 'in short') used as a discourse marker; 'словом' is in the instrumental case.
segments.p1-9-g150: 'Человек' is the nominative subject 'man/person'; 'устроен' is a short-passive/adjectival form (past participle used predicatively) meaning 'is arranged/constituted'; 'комически' is an adverb modifying the predicate, 'comically' or 'absurdly'.
segments.p1-9-g151: Во всем этом is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in all this' or 'throughout all of this'; 'во' is the variant of 'в' used before the consonant cluster, 'всем' = 'all', 'этом' = 'this' in the prepositional.
segments.p1-9-g152: Очевидно is an adverbial comment 'obviously' or 'clearly', modifying the whole clause that follows.
segments.p1-9-g153: 'Заключается' is the reflexive verb 'заключаться' in the present, 3rd person singular — 'consists in' / 'lies in' (the thing is contained in something); 'каламбур' is the nominative subject 'a pun'. Note the verb precedes the noun for stylistic effect.
segments.p1-9-g154: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'; 'дважды два четыре' is a compact numeral expression meaning 'two times two [equals] four' used as a noun phrase (dash marks the pause/apposition); 'все-таки' (here split between 'все' and 'таки') is an adverbial particle meaning 'after all' / 'nevertheless'; 'вещь' is 'thing' or 'matter'; 'пренесносная' is an archaic/intensifying adjective (somewhat ironic) meaning 'very/most notable' or 'extremely significant' — the diction is deliberately old-fashioned and emphatic.
segments.p1-9-g155: 'Дважды два четыре' repeats the numeral phrase as the topical subject (dash marks the appositive pause); 'ведь' is a particle meaning 'after all' / 'you see' that gives an explanatory or confirmatory tone; 'это' is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/that'.
segments.p1-9-g156: По моему мнению is the standard Russian idiom for 'in my opinion' — 'по' with the dative ('моему') + noun ('мнению') where 'по' means 'according to' or 'with respect to'.
segments.p1-9-g157: 'Только' is 'only/merely'; 'нахальство' is a noun meaning 'impudence' or 'cheekiness'; the clipped suffix '-с' is a colloquial/ironical ending (deliberately playful or archaic) attached for tone. The copula ('is') is omitted, as normal in present-tense Russian predicative phrases.
segments.p1-9-g133: 'Ведь' is a discourse particle meaning 'after all' or 'you know' and serves to emphasize the statement; 'он' is the nominative subject 'he'; 'чувствует' is present indicative, 3rd person singular of 'чувствовать' and here means 'feels' or 'has the impression' and introduces the subordinate clause (hence the comma before 'что').
segments.p1-9-g134: 'что' introduces the subordinate clause 'that'; 'как' here functions as a temporal conjunction 'as/when' with the sense 'as soon as'; 'найдет' is the perfective future, 3rd person singular of 'найти' meaning 'will find' or 'finds' in the sense of a completed finding. Together this clause means 'that as soon as he finds (it)'.
segments.p1-9-g135: 'так уж' is an emphatic combination ('then/so' + intensifier 'really/indeed'); the construction 'нечего будет + infinitive' is impersonal and means 'there will be nothing (to ...)' — here 'будет' is future of 'быть', and 'тогда' specifies time 'then'; 'отыскивать' is the imperfective infinitive 'to search for' and paired with 'нечего' expresses that no further searching will be possible or necessary.
segments.p1-9-g136: 'Работники' is the plural nominative noun 'workers' and functions as the main subject/topic of the sentence; the following comma signals that an adverbial participial phrase will qualify them.
segments.p1-9-g137: 'кончив' is the perfective adverbial participle (деепричастие) from 'кончить' meaning 'having finished'; 'работу' is the direct object in the accusative ('work' or 'the work'). The participle indicates the action completed prior to the main verbs that follow.
segments.p1-9-g138: 'по крайней мере' is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'at least' (the three words function together); 'деньги получат' is the main clause that follows: 'деньги' is the direct object 'money' and 'получат' is future, 3rd person plural of 'получить' meaning 'will receive' or 'will get'.
segments.p1-9-g139: 'в' is the preposition 'into/to'; 'кабачок' here is colloquial for a small tavern/pub (not the vegetable); 'пойдут' is future, 3rd person plural of 'пойти' meaning 'will go' or 'will set off'. This short clause expresses the next action the workers take.
segments.p1-9-g140: 'потом' is an adverb 'then/afterwards'; 'в' again 'into/to'; 'часть' here means a 'unit/section' (contextually a military or organizational unit); 'попадут' is future, 3rd person plural of 'попасть' meaning 'will get into' or 'will end up in'. The clause describes a subsequent result.
segments.p1-9-g141: The short exclamation 'ну вот и' is colloquial ('well, that's it' / 'and so'); 'занятия' is plural nominative meaning 'occupations' or 'activities'; 'на' with accusative 'неделю' expresses duration 'for a week'. Together: 'well, that's occupations/entertainment for a week.'
segments.p1-9-g142: 'А' is a contrastive particle here meaning 'but' or 'and' in a rhetorical question; 'человек' is a generic nominative noun 'person' or 'man' as the subject; 'куда' is the interrogative adverb 'to where'; 'пойдет' is future, 3rd person singular of 'пойти' meaning 'will go'. The clause is a short rhetorical question about the person's next move.
segments.p1-9-g143: The phrase По крайней мере is an idiom meaning “at least.” Каждый раз is an adverbial phrase “each time” modifying замечается. Замечается is impersonal/passive present (“is noticed” / “one notices”), and the clause в нем что-то неловкое means literally “in him something awkward [is]” (в нем = in him; что-то = something; неловкое = awkward), with the indefinite что-то as the subject of the predicate adjective. При достижении подобных целей is a circumstantial prepositional phrase with при + noun in the gerundial/prepositional form (достижении) meaning “in/when achieving such goals.”
segments.p1-9-g170: 'Ведь' is a discourse particle used to remind or appeal to a shared assumption — here 'after all' or 'you know'; it doesn't affect word order or verb morphology but signals the speaker's rhetorical stance.
segments.p1-9-g171: 'Может быть' is a fixed modal expression meaning 'perhaps' or 'might be'; grammatically it's composed of the present form 'может' (modal 'may') plus the infinitive 'быть', but together they function as an adverbial hedge.
segments.p1-9-g172: Literal clause: 'человек' (subject), 'любит' (present indicative 'loves'), and 'не одно благоденствие' where 'не' negates 'одно' making 'not only one' (= 'not only'); word order is standard S V O with the negation before the quantifier.
segments.p1-9-g173: Same as above: 'Может быть' functions as a modal hedge 'perhaps' and does not change verb agreement — it's an adverbial phrase.
segments.p1-9-g174: Structure: 'он' (subject, 'he' used generically), 'ровно настолько же' is a fixed comparative cluster meaning 'exactly to that same extent' with 'ровно' stressing exactness and 'же' adding emphasis/identity, then 'любит' (present 'loves') and 'страдание' (accusative object).
segments.p1-9-g175: Again, 'Может быть' = 'perhaps'; it's a sentence‑level modal, introducing uncertainty about the proposition that follows.
segments.p1-9-g176: Core predicate: 'страдание-то' — the enclitic particle '-то' attached to the noun gives contrastive/emphatic force ('suffering, for its part'); 'ему' is the dative pronoun marking the experiencer ('to him'); 'ровно настолько же' repeats the exact comparative 'exactly to the same degree'; 'и' here works like 'also/even' linking the adverbial quality to the predicate; 'выгодно' is a predicative adverb meaning 'advantageous/profitable' and governs the dative.
segments.p1-9-g177: 'Как благоденствие' is a standard comparative phrase 'as (much) as well‑being', with 'как' introducing the comparison and 'благоденствие' the standard of comparison.
segments.p1-9-g178: 'А' here is a contrastive conjunction often rendered 'but' or 'and' depending on tone; 'человек' is the generic subject; 'иногда' places the action in frequency ('sometimes'); 'ужасно' is an adverb meaning 'extremely/very' modifying 'любит'; 'любит' is present tense 'loves' and 'страдание' is the direct object.
segments.p1-9-g179: 'До страсти' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'to the point of passion'; 'до' with a noun in genitive indicates extent up to that point ('up to / to the degree of'), and 'страсти' is the genitive of 'страсть' ('passion').
segments.p1-9-g180: 'И это факт.' is a simple declarative clause: conjunction 'и' (= 'and'), demonstrative 'это' ('this/that'), and noun 'факт' ('fact') — a blunt reinforcement of the preceding statement.
segments.p1-9-g237: The phrase is an adjective + noun in the dative plural: домашним is the dative plural form of домашний and животным is the dative plural of животное, so they agree in case, number, and gender. Adjective-before-noun word order is normal; here the starred phrase and the following parenthetical act as a gloss or editorial note. The parenthetical (франц.) is an abbreviation meaning “French” (i.e., indicating the equivalent or source is French).
segments.p1-9-g206: "А между тем" is an introductory phrase meaning "and yet" / "meanwhile"; here it cushions the following assertion. "Я уверен" is literally "I am sure" (я + predicative adjective уверен), with no copula required in Russian present-tense statements.
segments.p1-9-g207: "что" introduces a subordinate clause ('that'). "человек" is the generic subject ('a person, man'). The preposition "от" governs the genitive "настоящего страдания" and here means "from / because of" (i.e. 'suffering as a cause').
segments.p1-9-g208: "то есть" is a fixed explanatory phrase meaning "that is" / "namely"; here it restates "настоящее страдание" by specifying it as "от разрушения и хаоса". The preposition "от" again takes the genitive.
segments.p1-9-g209: "Никогда не откажется" is a negated future meaning "will never refuse/renounce" (откажется = 3rd person singular future of the perfective reflexive verb отказаться). The negative construction "никогда не" places emphasis on the permanence of refusal.
segments.p1-9-g210: The dash sets up an emphatic statement equating the noun to what follows. "да ведь" is a colloquial emphatic particle combination meaning "after all" / "you see", introducing a confirmation. "это единственная причина сознания" is a nominal predicate: "this (is) the only cause of consciousness" ("есть" copula is omitted in present-tense Russian but implied).
segments.p1-9-g211: "Я хоть и доложил вначале" uses the concessive particle pair "хоть и" (= "although/even though") with the past-tense verb "доложил" ('I stated/reported') and the adverb "вначале" ('at first / at the beginning'). The literal sense: "I, although I did state at the start..."
segments.p1-9-g212: "что" again introduces reported content ("that"). "сознание" is the subject of the embedded clause ('consciousness').
segments.p1-9-g213: "по-моему" is an idiomatic parenthetical meaning "in my opinion"; grammatically it is composed of the preposition "по" + dative "моему" and modifies the clause as the speaker's comment.
segments.p1-9-g214: "есть величайшее для человека несчастие" is a predicative clause: "is the greatest misfortune for a person." Here "есть" functions as the copula (often optional in Russian), "величайшее" is the superlative adjective 'greatest', and "для человека" ('for a person') marks the beneficiary/affected party.
segments.p1-9-g215: "Но я знаю" contrasts the prior concessive remark with an assertion: "but I know..." — "но" (but), "я" (I), present-tense verb "знаю" (I know).
segments.p1-9-g216: "что человек его любит и не променяет ни на какие удовлетворения" is a compound clause: "that a person loves it and will not trade it for any satisfactions." "его" is the object pronoun referring back to "сознание"; "любит" is present-tense 'loves' (used here as a general/truth-of-human-nature statement); "не променяет" is future-tense (perfective) 'will not exchange/ trade away'; "ни на какие" is the negative intensifier meaning 'not for any (whatsoever)'.
segments.p1-9-g217: Сознание is a nominative abstract noun functioning as the subject of the sentence; it names the thing being predicated about.
segments.p1-9-g218: Например is a parenthetical adverb meaning “for example” and sets off an illustrative remark; it does not affect sentence agreement.
segments.p1-9-g219: Бесконечно is an adverbial intensifier here (“infinitely”), modifying the comparative выше, which is the comparative form meaning “higher” (i.e., superior to). Together they form the predicate “is infinitely higher.”
segments.p1-9-g220: Чем is the comparative particle “than” introducing the standard comparison; дважды два is the numeric expression “two times two” (i.e., 2×2) serving as the comparison target.
segments.p1-9-g158: "Дважды два четыре" is a nominalized arithmetic phrase functioning as the subject (literally “two times two [is] four”). "смотрит" is present-tense, 3rd person singular of смотреть, meaning ‘looks’ or ‘stares.’ "фертом" is an adverbial/qualifying word formed from the obscure noun ферт; Dostoevsky uses it adverbially to characterize the manner of the look (an archaic/obscure descriptive word, not a standard modern adverb).
segments.p1-9-g159: The verb "стоит" here means ‘stands’ (3rd person singular present) and takes the following adverbial phrase. "поперек вашей дороги" is a prepositional phrase (поперёк + genitive) meaning ‘across/obstructing your road’ (poperek with the genitive of дорога). "руки в боки" is an idiomatic descriptive phrase meaning ‘with (its) hands on its hips’ (руки is the noun phrase and в + accusative боки indicates position ‘to the sides’). The clause closes with "и плюется" — "и" = ‘and’, "плюется" (plеваться, 3rd sg. reflexive) = ‘spits’ (here with the sense of spitting in disgust).
segments.p1-9-g160: "Я" is the nominative pronoun ‘I.’ "согласен" is the short-form adjective (predicate) meaning ‘I agree’ or ‘I am in agreement’; short-form adjectives are used predicatively to express the speaker’s state.
segments.p1-9-g161: "что" introduces the content clause (‘that …’). The phrase "дважды два четыре" is treated as the clause’s subject (the arithmetic statement). The dash functions as a copula marker; "превосходная вещь" is a nominative predicate meaning ‘an excellent thing.’
segments.p1-9-g162: "но" = ‘but’. "если" introduces a conditional clause (‘if’). "уже" here is an adverbial intensifier meaning roughly ‘already’ or ‘once (you’re at it)’. "все" means ‘everything’ in this context, and "хвалить" is the infinitive ‘to praise’ — together: ‘if one is going to praise everything…’
segments.p1-9-g163: "то" is the correlative particle linking the conditional (‘then’). The particle "и" here functions as ‘even/also’ (emphatic). "дважды два пять" parallels the earlier arithmetic phrase (‘two times two equals five’). "премилая иногда вещица" is the predicate: премилая = ‘very charming/very dear’, иногда = ‘sometimes’, вещица = diminutive ‘little thing/trifle’ — together: ‘even that can sometimes be a delightful little thing.’
segments.p1-9-g164: "И" is a connective ‘and’ starting the question. "почему" = ‘why’. "вы" is the polite/plural ‘you’. "так" is an intensifier ‘so’. "твердо" is an adverb meaning ‘firmly’ or ‘resolutely’ — here qualifying the certainty of the addressees.
segments.p1-9-g165: Repeated intensification: the second "так" = ‘so’ again; "торжественно" is an adverb meaning ‘solemnly’ or ‘triumphantly’; "уверены" is the short-form plural adjective ‘are certain/sure’ agreeing with "вы" (you are so solemnly sure).
segments.p1-9-g166: "что" introduces the quoted content of the certainty (‘that …’). "только одно" = ‘only one’ (одно is neuter to agree with the implied neuter noun that follows). "нормальное и положительное" are coordinated adjectives in neuter singular describing that one thing: ‘the normal and the positive.’
segments.p1-9-g167: "одним словом" is an idiomatic parenthetical in the instrumental case meaning ‘in a word’ or ‘to put it briefly’ (literally, ‘with one word’); the dash sets it off as an aside.
segments.p1-9-g168: "только одно" repeats ‘only one’ and now is completed by the noun "благоденствие" (neuter, ‘well-being’ or ‘prosperity’). "человеку" is dative (‘to/for man/person’), so the phrase means ‘beneficial/advantageous to man.’ "выгодно" is the predicate meaning ‘is advantageous/profitable’ (impersonal predicate agreeing with the dative).
segments.p1-9-g169: The negative question begins with "Не" = ‘not’. "ошибается" (ошибаться) is present 3rd singular reflexive meaning ‘is mistaken’ or ‘makes a mistake’ (here used impersonally of reason). The enclitic "ли" turns it into a question. "разум-то" is the subject ‘reason’ with the emphatic particle "-то" (attached for contrast/emphasis). "в выгодах" is a prepositional phrase (в + plural) meaning ‘in matters of advantages/benefits’ — together: ‘Is reason mistaken regarding advantages?’
segments.p1-9-g191: Стою is 1st person singular present of стоять (“to stand”); the verb precedes the pronoun here for emphasis or abruptness. The pronoun я is postverbal, highlighting or contrasting the speaker; the whole short sentence is an emphatic, elliptical assertion of position.
segments.p1-9-g194: За governs a noun in the accusative and here means “for” or “in support of.” Свой is the reflexive possessive agreeing with the (implicit) subject я (“my/one's own”). Каприз is the direct object in accusative (same form as nominative). The phrase за то introduces the second coordinated object as “for that (fact/thing).”
segments.p1-9-g195: Чтоб is a colloquial/clausal form of чтобы introducing the desired content (“that…”/“so that…”). Он refers back to то (the thing just mentioned). Был is the past tense of быть used after чтоб to express a wished-for state (a subjunctive-like use). Мне is dative, indicating the person who should receive the guarantee. Гарантирован is a short passive participle used predicatively: 'to be guaranteed.'
segments.p1-9-g196: Когда introduces a temporal clause meaning “when” or more idiomatically “when necessary / when it becomes necessary.” Понадобится is an impersonal/perfective 3rd‑person singular future form of понадобиться, here functioning as 'will be needed' or simply 'is needed' in the idiomatic expression 'когда понадобится' = 'when required/whenever needed.'
segments.p1-9-g197: Страдание is a nominative noun functioning as the topic/subject of the clause ('suffering'). The comma marks it as the theme for the comment that follows.
segments.p1-9-g198: Например is an adverbial parenthetical meaning 'for example' that modifies the statement about suffering.
segments.p1-9-g199: В водевилях uses the preposition в with the prepositional plural (locative) водевилях to mean 'in vaudeville (shows).' Не допускается is a passive/impersonal construction meaning 'is not allowed/admitted' (допускаться + не = 'is not permitted').
segments.p1-9-g200: Я это знаю is a simple declarative: я (subject) + это (demonstrative object, 'this/that') + знаю (1st person present of знать, 'I know'). The clause confirms the speaker's knowledge of the preceding statement.
segments.p1-9-g201: В хрустальном дворце is a locative prepositional phrase ('in the crystal palace'). Оно is a neuter pronoun/dummy subject referring back to 'страдание' (or to the idea just mentioned); here it introduces the predication. И before немыслимо functions as an emphatic/modal particle 'even' or 'indeed.' Немыслимо is a neuter short adjective/adverbial predicate meaning 'unthinkable' or 'inconceivable.'
segments.p1-9-g202: Страдание есть сомнение uses the copula есть (present 'is') for added emphasis; страдание (nominative) is equated with сомнение (nominative predicate), 'suffering is doubt.'
segments.p1-9-g203: Есть отрицание repeats the copular construction in apposition: 'it is negation' (отрицание used as a philosophical predicate). The repetition stresses the author's equation of suffering with denial/negation.
segments.p1-9-g204: А is a contrasting conjunction 'but' or 'and' introducing the rhetorical question. Что ... за is an idiomatic combination meaning 'what sort of' or 'what kind of' (что is the interrogative element; за is part of the set phrase что за). Хрустальный дворец is the noun phrase being questioned ('what kind of crystal palace').
segments.p1-9-g205: В котором is the relative pronoun in the prepositional case agreeing with 'дворец' ('in which'). Можно is an impersonal/modal adverb meaning 'it is possible' or 'one could,' and усумниться is a perfective reflexive verb meaning 'to begin to doubt' or 'to have doubts.' Together they form the rhetorical 'in which one could (ever) begin to doubt?'
segments.p1-9-g102: Но is a conjunction meaning 'but' that links this sentence to the previous thought. Человек is the nominative subject ('man' or 'a person'), followed by существо in apposition ('a creature/being'); the two adjectives легкомысленное и неблаговидное are neuter singular to agree with существо (not with человек) and describe the creature. The final и coordinates this description with the next clause.
segments.p1-9-g103: может быть is a parenthetical modal phrase meaning 'perhaps' or 'may be'; grammatically it is formed from может (3rd-person of мочь) + быть but here functions idiomatically to mark possibility rather than literal ability.
segments.p1-9-g104: подобно takes the dative and means 'like' or 'similar to'; шахматному игроку is dative ('to/like a chess player'), with шахматному as an adjective meaning 'chess' and игроку the dative of 'player'.
segments.p1-9-g105: любит is 3rd-person singular present 'loves/likes'; только один ('only one') limits what is loved; процесс достижения цели is a noun phrase 'the process of attaining a goal' where достижения and цели are genitives modifying процесс (process of attainment/of the goal).
segments.p1-9-g106: а is a contrastive conjunction 'but' (introducing the contrast); не negates самую цель. самую is the feminine accusative form of самый used intensively here ('the very' / 'itself') and agrees with цель, so 'not the goal itself.'
segments.p1-9-g107: И кто знает is an idiomatic interrogative/indefinite 'and who knows' (i.e. 'who can say'). The parenthesis (поручиться нельзя) is an impersonal construction: поручиться (infinitive 'to vouch for') + нельзя ('it is not possible / one cannot'), meaning 'one cannot vouch for it' / 'one cannot be sure.'
segments.p1-9-g108: может быть — the same parenthetical modal 'perhaps / maybe' as in the previous sentence; idiomatic, not literal ability.
segments.p1-9-g109: что introduces the subordinate clause 'that'. и here is an emphatic particle ('even/indeed') attached to вся-то (вся + the emphatic particle то) — вся-то means 'the whole' with emphasis. цель is the noun 'purpose/goal' and на земле is a locative specifying 'on earth / in the world'.
segments.p1-9-g110: к которой is a relative pronoun in the dative ('to/for which') agreeing with цель and governed by the preposition к; человечество is the nominative subject 'humanity' and стремится is 3rd-person singular reflexive/inactional verb 'strives/aims for' (takes the prepositional/dative construction).
segments.p1-9-g111: только и заключается is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'consists only' or 'merely consists in' (заключаться is a reflexive verb 'to consist in / to lie in'). в одной этой беспрерывности is a prepositional phrase 'in this one continuity' (одной emphasises single/particular), and процесса достижения is the genitive 'of the process of attainment' modifying беспрерывности.
segments.p1-9-g112: иначе сказать is a parenthetical explanatory phrase 'to say otherwise / in other words' (иначе 'differently' + infinitive сказать 'to say'); в самой жизни is a prepositional phrase 'in life itself', with самой intensifying жизнь.
segments.p1-9-g113: а introduces a contrast 'but / and (on the other hand)'; не собственно means 'not properly / not strictly speaking' (собственно used adverbially), and в цели is 'in the goal' (prepositional).
segments.p1-9-g114: которая is the feminine relative pronoun 'which' referring back to цель and beginning the explanatory subordinate clause that follows.
segments.p1-9-g115: разумеется is a parenthetical adverb 'of course / naturally' used to signal that the following remark is to be taken as obvious or expected by the speaker.
segments.p1-9-g116: должна быть is a modal construction: должна (feminine) expresses obligation/necessity for the feminine noun цель ('must be'); не иное что is a somewhat inverted phrasing meaning 'nothing other than' (не + иное + что functioning collectively as 'nothing other than').
segments.p1-9-g117: как here introduces an example 'as / namely'; дважды два четыре is a colloquial, abbreviated arithmetic phrase literally 'twice two four' understood as 'two times two equals four' (the verb 'to be' is omitted but implied).
segments.p1-9-g118: то есть is the fixed explanatory phrase 'that is / i.e.'; есть is the copula 'is' (explicit here) and формула is the predicate noun 'a formula.'
segments.p1-9-g119: а ведь is a contrastive/emphatic linkage 'but after all'; дважды два четыре есть reiterates 'two times two equals' with есть as copula; уже не жизнь means 'already not life' (уже indicating that it has become no longer life).
segments.p1-9-g120: господа is a vocative/exclamatory address 'gentlemen' (used to address the audience or listeners).
segments.p1-9-g121: а is the contrastive conjunction 'but'; начало is a nominative noun 'the beginning' and смерти is the genitive 'of death' — together 'the beginning of death.'
segments.p1-9-g122: По крайней мере is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'at least / at any rate'. Человек here is generic singular used to mean 'people / man' in general; всегда 'always' and как-то 'somehow' modify боялся (past tense 'was afraid'). Этого is genitive demonstrative 'of this/that' modifying the following arithmetic phrase (дважды два четыре is the object).
segments.p1-9-g123: а is contrastive 'but'; я is the first-person singular subject 'I'; и here is emphatic 'even' (i.e. 'even now'); теперь is 'now'; боюсь is present 'am afraid'.
segments.p1-9-g124: Положим here is an introductory imperative/optative 'let us suppose / assume' used to set up a hypothetical case; it is not literal 'to lay/put' but the conversational/essayistic 'suppose'.
segments.p1-9-g125: человек is again the generic subject 'a man / one'; только и делает is the idiom 'does nothing but' (только и placing emphasis on the sole activity) and делает is the present-tense verb 'does'.
segments.p1-9-g126: что here introduces the clause 'that' (explaining what he does): отыскивает is the imperfective present 'searches out / seeks for', эти is the demonstrative 'these' and the arithmetic phrase is the object.
segments.p1-9-g127: океаны is the accusative plural 'oceans' (direct object) and переплывает is the present tense singular of переплывать 'crosses (by swimming / crosses over)', with the subject implied from the prior clause (he). The inversion (object before verb) is a stylistic, telegraphic listing of feats.
segments.p1-9-g128: жизнью is in the instrumental case 'with (his) life' meaning 'sacrifices his life'; жертвует is present-tense 'sacrifices' (takes instrumental); в этом отыскивании is a prepositional phrase 'in this searching' where отыскивании is the noun/gerund 'the act of searching'.
segments.p1-9-g129: но is the adversative conjunction 'but'; отыскать is the perfective infinitive 'to find' (contrasting the continued search with the completed act of finding).
segments.p1-9-g130: действительно is an adverb 'indeed / really' intensifying the following infinitive; найти is also a perfective infinitive 'to find' (synonymous with отыскать but used here for emphasis: 'actually to find').
segments.p1-9-g131: ей-богу is an interjectional oath 'by God' used parenthetically as an exclamation; the hyphens mark it off as an inserted oath.
segments.p1-9-g132: как-то is the colloquial adverb 'somehow' (как + particle то) and боится is 3rd-person singular present 'is afraid' — the subject ('he' / 'the man') is understood from earlier. Together: 'he somehow is afraid.'
segments.p1-9-g181: Тут уж и — a compressed introductory cluster: Тут (location/‘at this point’) + уж (emphatic particle) + и (here meaning ‘even’) set the tone; со всемирной историей is an instrumental phrase introduced by со (variant of с used before certain consonant clusters) modifying справляться (infinitive). Справляться нечего is an impersonal construction where the infinitive + нечего expresses futility: ‘there is no use/there is nothing to be done (about)’. The semicolon separates the general remark from the following address to the reader.
segments.p1-9-g182: Спроcите — imperative (2nd person plural/formal) of спросить ‘ask’; себя самого is the reflexive accusative + сам (intensifier) and literally means ‘your very self’ or ‘yourself, personally’. The comma marks a brief address before the conditional clause that follows.
segments.p1-9-g183: Если только — conditional/headed concessive phrase meaning ‘if only/provided that’; вы человек is the main descriptive clause (‘if you are a human being’), with вы in the formal/plural. И хоть сколько-нибудь жили: и adds ‘and’, хоть сколько-нибудь is an idiomatic cluster meaning ‘even to any extent / at least a little’ (хоть = ‘even/at least’, сколько — ‘how much/as much’, -нибудь indefinite), and жили is the past tense plural of жить agreeing with вы.
segments.p1-9-g184: Что же касается до моего личного мнения is a formulaic phrase meaning ‘as for my personal opinion’. Что же — introductory ‘as for / well’, где же adds emphasis; касается (3rd sg) ‘concerns’ normally governs genitive, and here до is used colloquially with касаться + genitive to mean ‘as to / concerning’. Моего личного мнения is genitive ‘of my personal opinion’.
segments.p1-9-g185: То introduces the result/statement (‘then / as for that’). Любить is an infinitive used impersonally to state a general judgement: ‘to love / to have a penchant for’. Только одно благоденствие is the object: только ‘only’, одно emphasises singularity, благоденствие ‘well‑being/prosperity’. Даже как-то и неприлично is a colloquial evaluative tag: даже ‘even’, как-то + то (here functioning as a fused adverbial ‘somehow’), и is a mild reinforcing particle, and неприлично is the predicate meaning ‘unseemly / indecent’ — together: ‘is somehow even unseemly’.
segments.p1-9-g186: Хорошо ли — Хорошо is an adjective used adverbially (‘good’/‘well’), and the enclitic ли turns it into part of the correlative ‘whether ... or ...’ construction; the comma marks the first half of the pair.
segments.p1-9-g187: Дурно ли — parallel to the previous segment: дурно (‘bad’/‘badly’) with ли completes the correlative ‘whether good or bad’. The short clauses are rhetorical, introducing the speaker’s concession.
segments.p1-9-g188: Но introduces a contrast (‘but’). Разломать is a perfective infinitive ‘to break apart’ functioning as the notional subject of the impersonal predicate приятно; иногда ‘sometimes’ and что-нибудь ‘something/anything’ (indefinite object) specify frequency and object, and тоже ‘also’ with очень приятно ‘very pleasant’ gives the evaluative conclusion: ‘but sometimes breaking something is also very pleasant.’
segments.p1-9-g189: Я ведь тут собственно не за страдание стою is a first‑person present statement: Я (I) + ведь (after all / you know) + тут (here) + собственно (actually/properly) give pragmatic colouring; не за страдание (за + accusative) functions as a short phrase ‘for suffering’ in the sense ‘in favour of / on behalf of suffering’, and стою (1st sg present of стоять) is used figuratively ‘I stand / I take my position’ — ‘I am not, actually, standing here for suffering.’
segments.p1-9-g190: Да и не за благоденствие is a brief addition: да и functions like ‘and besides / nor’, combining with не за (not for) + благоденствие (well‑being/prosperity) to deny the opposite motive. The clause is elliptical (no verb) and relies on the previous стою for its verb.
segments.p1-9-g221: После is a preposition meaning 'after' that governs the following numeral phrase. 'дважды двух' is a numeric expression ('two times two') presented after после (so the whole phrase means 'after two times two'); уж is an emphatic particle meaning 'already' that attaches to the phrase for immediacy.
segments.p1-9-g222: разумеется is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally' and stands detached by commas to comment on the whole clause.
segments.p1-9-g223: This is an impersonal negative construction: нечего (nothing/to be done) plus the verb останется (future, 3rd person singular of остаться) with the negative particle не. Together 'ничего не останется' = 'there will remain nothing' / 'nothing will be left.'
segments.p1-9-g224: не только is the first half of the correlative 'not only ... but ...' Here it modifies the infinitive делать ('to do'); the infinitive serves as the complement of нечего in the impersonal construction ('nothing to do').
segments.p1-9-g225: но contrasts with the previous part; даже и is an emphatic combination meaning 'even' (и reinforces даже) and precedes the infinitive узнавать ('to learn/find out'), completing the correlative: 'not only to do, but even to learn.'
segments.p1-9-g226: Все is the head pronoun 'all/everything' and introduces the relative clause that follows; here it means 'everything' (i.e. 'all that').
segments.p1-9-g227: The relative clause 'что тогда можно будет' modifies 'всё'. что = 'that/which'; тогда = 'then' (temporal); можно будет is an impersonal modal with будущее 'будет' meaning 'will be possible' (literally 'it will be possible then').
segments.p1-9-g228: это introduces the explanation ('that is:'); the dash sets off an infinitival clause: заткнуть (perfective infinitive 'to plug/shut up') takes 'свои пять чувств' as its object ('one's five senses'); и links the second infinitive погрузиться ('to immerse/plunge oneself') which governs в + созерцание ('into contemplation').
segments.p1-9-g229: Ну is a discourse particle 'well' used to open or soften the remark; it does not affect sentence grammar beyond marking tone.
segments.p1-9-g230: а is a contrastive conjunction ('and/while/but'); при сознании is a prepositional phrase with при + prepositional case meaning 'in the presence of / when one has consciousness' (i.e. 'if one is conscious'); хоть и is a concessive conjunction 'although/even though'; тот же результат is the noun phrase 'the same result' and выходит (present tense of выходить) here means 'turns out / obtains' — 'although the same result follows.'
segments.p1-9-g231: то есть is a parenthetical 'that is' / 'i.e.'; тоже 'also' links to the previous idea; будет нечего делать is the same impersonal future construction as before: 'there will be nothing to do.'
segments.p1-9-g232: но 'but'; по крайней мере is the fixed adverbial phrase 'at least' (по + крайняя мера); самого себя is a reflexive intensified object 'oneself (the very self)'; иногда = 'sometimes'; можно is impersonal 'it is possible/one can'; посечь here is an infinitive meaning 'to flog/beat' (literally 'to cut/whip') with себя as its object — 'one can sometimes flog oneself.'
segments.p1-9-g233: а is a mild contrastive 'and / but'; это = 'this/that' refers back to the preceding 'flogging oneself'; все-таки (here split into все + таки) is the adverbial particle 'after all / still'; подживляет is 3rd person singular present of подживлять meaning roughly 'to keep (someone) a little alive' or 'to help one endure' — 'and that, after all, keeps one going.'
segments.p1-9-g234: Хоть и is a concessive conjunction 'though / even if'; ретроградно is an adverb ('retrograde' / 'backward-looking') describing the implied state ('though [it is] retrograde').
segments.p1-9-g235: а introduces contrast 'but'; всё же (here split as все + же) is a common particle meaning 'still / nevertheless'; лучше is the comparative 'better' and completes the predication: 'but still better.'
segments.p1-9-g236: чем marks the comparison 'than'; ничего is the negative pronoun 'nothing' used as the standard of comparison: 'better than nothing.'

sentences.p1-9-s13: I agree: man is an animal, predominantly a creative one, compelled to strive consciously for a goal and to practise the engineering art — that is, to be forever and uninterruptedly making a road for himself, even to anywhere at all.
sentences.p1-9-s14: But it is precisely for that reason, perhaps, that he sometimes feels like swerving aside — that he is fated to force his way through that road, and moreover, perhaps because, however foolish the immediate doer may be in general, it still sometimes occurs to him that the road, it turns out, almost always leads anywhere, and that the main thing is not where it leads but that it should simply go on, and that the well‑behaved child, neglecting engineering skill, should not give himself up to ruinous idleness, which, as is known, is the mother of all vices.
sentences.p1-9-s15: Man loves to create and to lay out roads; this is indisputable.
sentences.p1-9-s16: But why, then, does he, to the point of passion, also love destruction and chaos?
sentences.p1-9-s9: After all, this is for the time being just one of your assumptions.
sentences.p1-9-s10: Let us suppose that this is a law of logic, but perhaps not a law of humanity at all.
sentences.p1-9-s11: You, perhaps, think, gentlemen, that I am insane?
sentences.p1-9-s22: Here (are) ants of a completely different sort.
sentences.p1-9-s23: They have one remarkable building of the same kind, forever indestructible — an anthill.
sentences.p1-9-s24: The venerable ants began from the anthill, and, probably, will end with the anthill, which brings great honour to their constancy and positiveness.
sentences.p1-9-s1: Gentlemen, I'm joking, of course, and I myself know that I'm joking badly, but after all one can't take everything for a joke.
sentences.p1-9-s2: I may, perhaps, be joking, grinding my teeth.
sentences.p1-9-s3: Gentlemen, questions torment me; allow me to resolve them.
sentences.p1-9-s4: For example, you want to wean a person off old habits and reform his will in accordance with the requirements of science and sound common sense.
sentences.p1-9-s5: But how do you know that a person can not only be changed, but should be remade that way?
sentences.p1-9-s6: From what do you infer that human desire must be reformed so (much)?
sentences.p1-9-s7: In short, why do you know that such a reform will actually bring a person benefit?
sentences.p1-9-s8: And, if we're being frank, why are you so certainly convinced that not going against the real, normal advantages guaranteed by the arguments of reason and arithmetic is indeed always advantageous for man and is a law for all humanity?
sentences.p1-9-s18: But about that I myself particularly want to say two words.
sentences.p1-9-s19: Isn't it perhaps for that reason that he loves destruction and chaos so much (for it is indisputable that he sometimes loves it very much, that's how it is), namely that he himself instinctively fears reaching the goal and completing the edifice he is creating?
sentences.p1-9-s20: How do you know? Maybe he loves the building only from afar, and by no means up close; maybe he only likes creating it, and not living in it, leaving it afterwards to 'aux animaux domestiques' — for example to ants, rams, and so on.
sentences.p1-9-s33: He likes achievement, but to actually attain it — not quite anymore; and that, of course, is terribly funny.
sentences.p1-9-s34: In a word, man is comically arranged; in all this, obviously, there lies a pun.
sentences.p1-9-s35: But two times two equals four — after all, a terribly significant matter.
sentences.p1-9-s36: Two times two equals four — you know, that, in my opinion, is simply impudence.
sentences.p1-9-s29: After all, he feels that as soon as he finds it, there will be nothing left to search for.
sentences.p1-9-s30: The workers, having finished their work, at the very least will get paid, go to a tavern, then end up in the unit — well, that's activities for a week.
sentences.p1-9-s31: But where will the man go?
sentences.p1-9-s32: At least, each time there is noticed in him something awkward when attaining such goals.
sentences.p1-9-s41: After all, perhaps a person doesn't love only well‑being?
sentences.p1-9-s42: Perhaps he loves suffering just as much?
sentences.p1-9-s43: Perhaps suffering is exactly as advantageous to him as well‑being?
sentences.p1-9-s44: But a person sometimes very much loves suffering — to the point of passion — and that's a fact.
sentences.p1-9-s55: And yet I am sure that a person will never give up true suffering, that is, destruction and chaos.
sentences.p1-9-s56: Suffering — after all, it is the sole cause of consciousness.
sentences.p1-9-s57: Though I did say at the outset that consciousness, in my opinion, is the greatest misfortune for a person, I know that a person loves it and will not exchange it for any satisfactions.
sentences.p1-9-s58: Consciousness, for example, is infinitely higher than two times two.
sentences.p1-9-s37: “Two times two equals four” glares at you in a ferte-like way, stands right across your path with its hands on its hips, and spits.
sentences.p1-9-s38: I agree that ‘two times two equals four’ is an excellent thing; but if one is already going to praise everything, then even ‘two times two equals five’ can sometimes be a most charming little thing.
sentences.p1-9-s39: And why are you so firmly, so solemnly sure that only one thing — the normal and the positive, in short — is beneficial to man?
sentences.p1-9-s40: Might reason itself be mistaken about what is advantageous?
sentences.p1-9-s52: for my own whim and for that it should be guaranteed to me when necessary.
sentences.p1-9-s53: Suffering, for example, is not permitted in vaudeville; I know that.
sentences.p1-9-s54: In the crystal palace it is even inconceivable: suffering is doubt, is negation; and what sort of crystal palace is there in which one could begin to doubt?
sentences.p1-9-s25: But man is a frivolous, unseemly creature and, perhaps like a chess-player, loves only the process of attaining a goal, and not the goal itself.
sentences.p1-9-s26: And who knows (one cannot vouch for it), perhaps the whole purpose on earth that humanity strives for consists only in this uninterruptedness of the process of attainment — in other words, in life itself — and not properly in the goal, which, of course, must be nothing other than 'two times two equals four', that is a formula; but after all, 'two times two equals four' is already not life, gentlemen, but the beginning of death.
sentences.p1-9-s27: At any rate, people have always somehow been afraid of that 'two times two equals four', and I even now am afraid.
sentences.p1-9-s28: Suppose a man does nothing but seek out these 'two times two equals four' — he crosses oceans, sacrifices his life in that seeking — but to find them, truly to find them, — by God — he somehow is afraid.
sentences.p1-9-s45: There's no point even trying to cope with world history here; ask yourself, if you are a human being and have lived at all.
sentences.p1-9-s46: As for my personal opinion, I think it is somehow improper to love only prosperity alone.
sentences.p1-9-s47: Whether good or bad, but sometimes it is also very pleasant to break something.
sentences.p1-9-s48: I'm not really standing here for suffering, nor for prosperity either.
sentences.p1-9-s59: Once 'two times two' is already settled, of course there will be nothing left — not only to do, but not even to learn.
sentences.p1-9-s60: All that will be possible then is to stop up one's five senses and immerse oneself in contemplation.
sentences.p1-9-s61: Well, and with consciousness — even though the same result follows (that is, there will also be nothing to do) — at least one can sometimes flog oneself, and that after all keeps one going.
sentences.p1-9-s62: Though retrograde, it is still better than nothing.
